title: Published Contributor – Innovation at the Back of the Bus
situation: Andrea Silverstone and Carrie McManus were writing 'Innovation at the Back of the Bus: A Defence of Innovation in the Civil Society Sector' — a book designed to challenge large organizations to stop treating innovation as an afterthought and make it foundational to their work. They sought contributors who could bring credible practitioner perspective on why innovation fails in large, structured organizations.
obstacle: The core challenge was translating years of lived experience navigating innovation resistance inside large financial institutions into a coherent, intellectually honest argument — one that would resonate beyond financial services and speak to the structural and cultural barriers that suppress innovation across sectors.
actions: Contributed a chapter examining the structural barriers to innovation in large private-sector organizations
Drew on direct experience leading transformation, insight, and strategy functions inside major financial institutions
Articulated why innovation fails in most large organizations: bureaucracy, misaligned incentives, risk aversion, legacy governance, and the absence of structural enablers
Developed a framework for diagnosing innovation blockers and designing enabling infrastructure
Explored the difference between innovation activity and innovation progress
Examined the role of leadership behaviour, autonomy, decision rights, and culture in enabling or suppressing innovation
results: Published as a named contributor ('with Adam Waldman') alongside authors Andrea Silverstone and Carrie McManus
Book is publicly available and purchasable through Sagesse (a Domestic Violence Prevention Society)
Established a verifiable, public thought-leadership credential in innovation and organizational design
Contribution bridges private-sector strategy experience with broader organizational and social sector thinking
resumeBullet: Named chapter contributor to 'Innovation at the Back of the Bus' (Silverstone & McManus, Sagesse) — examining structural barriers to innovation in large organizations and frameworks for enabling it.
fullStory: I was invited to contribute to 'Innovation at the Back of the Bus' — a book by Andrea Silverstone and Carrie McManus defending innovation as a foundational organizational imperative, not a side project. My chapter drew directly on what I had spent years observing and fighting inside large financial institutions: the structural, cultural, and governance conditions that make innovation nearly impossible — and what it would actually take to fix them. The argument wasn't theoretical. It came from watching brilliant people with good ideas get absorbed, diluted, or ignored by organizations optimized for execution, not reinvention. The book is published, purchasable, and my name is on the cover. That's not something I can say about many things in my career — and it matters.
